title: Speculum Guy de Warwick
summary: The checked-in CME00039 XML represents the University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse text keyed from Georgiana Lea Morrill's 1898 EETS edition Speculum Gy de Warewyke. Morrill's 1,034-line reading text follows National Library of Scotland Adv.MS.19.2.1 (the Auchinleck Manuscript) as its continuous base, collates five further witnesses, and supplies its damaged conclusion chiefly from Cambridge University Library MS Dd.11.89. Four additional witnesses now known to scholarship were not used by Morrill. A complete scan of the printed edition and the complete 701-image inventory publicly exposed by the NLS Auchinleck project are cached; the latter remains incomplete as physical-object documentation. One Bodleian image immediately preceding the work is cached as a zero-overlap selection. A complete Manchester viewer is public but was not systematically extracted because the provider's exact terms prohibit that use; no complete public surrogate was verified for the remaining witnesses.
review_notes: The intellectual subject is the anonymous Middle English verse homily now generally called Speculum Guy de Warwick, not the Guy of Warwick romance, Alcuin's Latin Liber de virtutibus et vitiis, any manuscript miscellany, Morrill's edition, the U-M encoding, the repository XML, or a facsimile.

The XML has 1,073 L elements: 1,033 direct poem lines corresponding to Morrill's numbered lines 1–1034 with numbered line 14 absent, and forty witness-only or variant lines nested in apparatus notes. Twelve of those forty form Harley 525's replacement ending after Morrill line 828.

Morrill's six sigla are A1 = Adv.MS.19.2.1, R = Royal 17 B XVII, H1 = Harley 1731, A2 = Arundel 140, D = Dd.11.89, and H2 = Harley 525. Her lost Worsley 67 and empty Bodley 1731 references are not counted as additional extant witnesses.

Complete digital coverage is always scoped to the provider object exposed on 2026-07-12. The NLS project omits physical surfaces; Bodleian exposes only one selected leaf; Manchester's full viewer is not a reusable download authorization.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00039

TEMPHEAD records review on 2016-07-26 by lattaj with responsibility MURP; this is not evidence of the initial keying date.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME digital text CME00039

date_statements.label: documented XML review
date_statements.notes: The revision entry documents review rather than initial creation.

notes: The encoding includes the edition title page, Morrill's one-paragraph summary, the edited poem on printed pp. 3–45, marginal abstracts, and the footnote apparatus; it omits the introduction, prefatory matter, notes after the text, glossary, and index.
The forty L elements nested in NOTE are variant or witness-only readings and must not be added to the 1,033 direct reading-text lines.

entities.label: Georgiana Lea Morrill's Speculum Gy de Warewyke (1898)

date_statements.label: printing of edited text
date_statements.notes: Morrill's preface says the text was printed in May 1896; the completed volume bears the date 1898.

bibliographic.citation: Georgiana Lea Morrill, ed., Speculum Gy de Warewyke: An English Poem; with Introduction, Notes, and Glossary; Here for the First Time Printed and First Edited from the Manuscripts, Early English Text Society, Extra Series 75 (London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co., 1898).
bibliographic.title: Speculum Gy de Warewyke
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Extra Series 75
bibliographic.publisher: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co. for the Early English Text Society

notes: The edited poem occupies pp. 3–45; critical and explanatory notes occupy pp. 47–102, followed by editions cited, glossary, and an index of names.

Morrill calls the edition non-diplomatic and not distinctively critical: it follows A1 while correcting palpable error, supplies damaged or missing matter, and presents five comparison witnesses selectively in the apparatus.

holding.notes: The Speculum is copied by Auchinleck Scribe 2 at ff. 39ra–48r and ends imperfectly on a narrow surviving stub.

notes: Morrill selected this oldest approximately complete witness as base.

holding.notes: The second scribe copies the work at ff. 44r–53r. The witness omits Morrill lines 459–814 and replaces lines 829–840 with a twelve-line ending followed by the colophon Speculum Gydonis de Warewyke secundum Alquinum heremitam.

notes: The manuscript's former binding is separately cataloged as Harley 525/1.

holding.notes: The work occupies ff. 268r–275r, is titled and closed Alquyne, and is described as 971 lines.

notes: The codex is associated by the catalog with Bedfordshire; this is not transferred to the work's place of composition.

No complete local bundle was made. Whole-object supply and publication permission should be requested separately.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Six manuscripts were collated; A1 is the oldest and approximately complete but physically damaged at the end.
editorial_practices.representation: A1 supplies the continuous base, with comparison witnesses in notes and supplied readings in brackets.
editorial_practices.description: Morrill follows the Auchinleck witness as base except where she judges it palpably erroneous or physically defective.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Five comparison witnesses preserve substantive variants, lacunae, interpolations, and alternate conclusions.
editorial_practices.representation: Variants marked A2, D, H1, H2, and R appear in footnotes; purely orthographic and phonetic differences are generally omitted.
editorial_practices.description: The apparatus is selective rather than exhaustive and sometimes prints multi-line witness alternatives inside notes.
editorial_practices.scope: Edited poem and footnote apparatus on pp. 3–45

editorial_practices.representation: Expanded letters are italicized in the reading text and apparatus.
editorial_practices.description: Morrill expands abbreviations rather than reproducing signs diplomatically, and the XML preserves expansion italics as HI REND=italic.
editorial_practices.scope: Edited poem and apparatus on pp. 3–45

editorial_practices.source_feature: Manuscript punctuation, paragraphing, word division, and verse presentation
editorial_practices.representation: Editorial punctuation and set-in divisions are supplied; the A1 pilcrow is reproduced separately.
editorial_practices.description: The printed syntax and sectional layout are editorial and should not be treated as a diplomatic record of A1 punctuation.

editorial_practices.representation: Ordinary capitalization is regularized; large manuscript initials are distinguished.
editorial_practices.description: Morrill regularizes capitals while retaining a limited signal for manuscript display initials.

editorial_practices.source_feature: The 1898 volume contains a long introduction, poem and apparatus, post-text notes, editions list, glossary, and index.
editorial_practices.representation: The XML selects the title page, Morrill's summary, poem, marginal abstracts, and inline apparatus while omitting the rest of the volume.
editorial_practices.description: The encoding is a substantial but bounded edition-derived selection rather than a digital edition of the complete printed book.

evidence.citation: A. S. G. Edwards, ‘The Speculum Guy de Warwick and Lydgate's Guy of Warwick: The Non-Romance Middle English Tradition,’ in Guy of Warwick: Icon and Ancestor (D. S. Brewer, 2007), pp. 81–93
evidence.summary: Identifies the anonymous 1,034-line couplet verse homily, argues for probable early-fourteenth-century composition, enumerates ten witnesses and their textual extents, summarizes the contents, and treats direct dependence on Alcuin as tenuous beyond the opening portion.

open_questions.question: Can complete object-level facsimiles be obtained for Royal 17 B XVII, Harley 1731, Arundel 140, Dd.11.89, Harley 525, Add 36983, St John's MS 256, and Bodleian Add. C. 220?
next_steps: Ask each holding institution whether complete existing photography, legacy microfilm, or an unindexed internal surrogate exists.

For each order request binding exteriors, boards or covers, spine, pastedowns, endleaves, blanks, every folio and stub, adjacent works, former binding components where separate, and edge views if offered—not only target-work leaves.
Map the target work separately by exact folio and image sequence after receipt.
Confirm private retention and publication or redistribution terms separately before caching supplied files.
notes: No complete manuscript surrogate from these institutions was cached. The Bodleian bundle is only the provider's single selected image and has zero work overlap.

open_questions.question: Can NLS supply a complete physical-object sequence for Adv.MS.19.2.1 beyond the legacy project's 701 exposed images?
next_steps: Request binding exteriors, boards, spine, pastedowns, all endleaves and blanks, every leaf and stub, detached fragments, and edge views if available.
Reconcile supplied physical sequence with the 701-image project inventory and retain both coverage claims separately.
Obtain written retention and reuse terms for any supplied files.

open_questions.question: Will Manchester authorize or supply a retainable complete English MS 50 image sequence despite the public viewer's prohibition on systematic extraction?
next_steps: Request a complete supplied object sequence through Imaging Services rather than extracting the viewer.
Ask for written terms covering private research retention, automated processing, quotation, publication, and redistribution separately.
Ask which item-level or general Creative Commons statement controls and how it interacts with the exact site terms.

open_questions.question: Why does the XML contain 1,033 direct reading-text L elements for Morrill's numbered lines 1–1034, with numbered line 14 absent, while also encoding forty variant lines inside apparatus notes?
next_steps: Collate the XML line by line against the cached images of Morrill pp. 3–45 and distinguish an edition numbering anomaly from a keying omission.
Ask U-M whether the structure is inherited from an earlier source package or known correction log.
Continue excluding NOTE descendants from reading-text line counts.

open_questions.question: Which 1896 printing appeared first: Carl Horstmann's Royal-based text in Yorkshire Writers, volume II, or Morrill's text printed in May 1896 before her completed 1898 volume?
next_steps: Establish issue dates from publisher, society, review, or correspondence evidence rather than relying on year-only title pages.
Until resolved, do not repeat Morrill's title-page claim that her text was the first printed edition as a modern catalog fact.

open_questions.question: What initial keying method, source copy, correction history, and source-file reuse terms apply to the U-M CME00039 encoding?
next_steps: Request item-level production metadata and any downloadable source package from U-M.
Keep the 2016 TEMPHEAD entry scoped to review rather than initial creation.
Compare XML and print before labeling structural differences intentional.

open_questions.question: Does the NLS current general PSI reuse policy supersede or modify the legacy Auchinleck project's personal-use and no-publication terms?
next_steps: Ask NLS for a written ruling specific to the 701 project images and locally retained hard-linked bundle.
Keep the bundle private and unstaged until clarified.
Record permission to retain separately from permission to publish or redistribute.
